mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant negative skew (p > 0.05) during triggered months across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both exceed their 4-quarter moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.1) in quarterly regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, causing immediate negative equity momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of declining INDPRO observations during tightening periods, or regime interaction coefficient is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment create a stagflationary pressure that forces equity risk premium expansion, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect produces positive 20-day returns in >50% of triggered instances, or the cross-term coefficient flips sign in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.3 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment, which reduces construction sector earnings and drags down broad market returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where housing starts drop below the threshold, or threshold regression shows no structural break.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and consumer confidence, generating positive short-term equity momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with payroll growth across a 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate during a post-shock recovery phase confirms labor market normalization, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of recovery-phase declining unemployment periods, or the regime-specific signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05).
